data.title: J&J, AbbVie get EU endorsement for Imbruvica's expanded approval
data.description:
Johnson & Johnson (NYSE:JNJ) said that an advisory committee of the EU drug regulator, the European Medicines Agency (EMA), endorsed a label expansion for its and AbbVie's (NYSE:ABBV) cancer drug, Imbruvica.

The EMA’s Committee for Medicinal Products for Human Use (CHMP) has issued a positive opinion recommending marketing authorization for an extended indication of Imbruvica to treat frontline mantle cell lymphoma.

This includes its use in combination with rituximab, cyclophosphamide, doxorubicin, vincristine, and prednisolone, alternating with R-DHAP without ibrutinib, followed by ibrutinib monotherapy for adults with previously untreated mantle cell lymphoma eligible for autologous stem cell transplant.

The extended indication is based on data from a late stage trial of the drug, the company added.

data.title: KindlyMD and Nakamoto announce additional $51.5 million in PIPE financing to support bitcoin treasury efforts
data.description:
• Kindly MD (NASDAQ:NAKA), which previously announced its anticipated merger with Nakamoto Holdings, announced on Friday the close of an additional $51.5 million in private placement in public equity (PIPE) to support its efforts to establish a Bitcoin treasury. 
  • To date, KindlyMD has raised a total of approximately $563 million in PIPE financing and $763 million, including convertible notes.
  • The transaction includes $51.5 million in gross proceeds from a fully committed PIPE financing priced at $5.00 per share and consisting of common stock in KindlyMD.
  • The net proceeds from the PIPE financing are intended to be used by KindlyMD to purchase Bitcoin and for working capital and general corporate purposes. The PIPE financing is expected to close concurrently with the merger.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: ArcelorMittal to sell operations in Bosnia, Herzegovina to Pavgord Group
data.description:
Steel and mining company ArcelorMittal (OTCPK:AMSYF) announced on Friday the sale of its operations in Bosnia and Herzegovina to Pavgord Group.

The sale consists of ArcelorMittal Zenica, an integrated steel plant, and ArcelorMittal Prijedor, an iron ore mining business.

Net of sale proceeds, the company expects to record a non-cash loss on disposal of ~$0.2B.

"After a thorough strategic review, the company concluded that a sale is the best solution for the development of the business and its people," said the company in a statement.

The deal is expected to close in the third quarter.

data.title: Jackpot Digital signs agreement with Paragon Casino Resort
data.description:
• Jackpot Digital (OTCQB:JPOTF) has entered into an agreement to install two of the Company's Jackpot Blitz® ETGs at Paragon Casino Resort located in Marksville, Louisiana, U.S.A.
  • The Co. has received the required gaming license and certification from the Tunica-Biloxi Tribal Gaming Commission and the Louisiana Indian Gaming Unit, which authorize the operation of electronic gaming devices within tribal jurisdictions.
  • The Jackpot Blitz® is a fully automated, dealerless poker electronic table game featuring a 75-inch touchscreen tabletop that seamlessly blends traditional poker gameplay with cutting-edge technology.
  • The ETG is designed to optimize operational efficiency for casino operators, while delivering an engaging and interactive experience for players.

data.title: Moleculin Biotech prices $5.9M public offering; shares down about 47%
data.description:
• Moleculin Biotech (NASDAQ:MBRX) is offering 16.08M shares or pre-funded warrants and Series E warrants to purchase up to 48.24M shares at $0.37/unit.
  • Series E warrants have a $0.37 exercise price, are exercisable upon shareholder approval, and expire five years after initial exercise.
  • Gross proceeds are expected to be ~$5.9M.
  • Closing is anticipated on or about June 23, 2025.
  • Funds will support clinical development of Annamycin and other drug candidates, preclinical research, and working capital.
  • MBRX shares slump nearly 47% on Friday.
  • Source: Press release

data.title: Stock market opens higher as Waller hints at potential rate cuts from July
data.description:
The stock market inched up on Friday, as Federal Reserve Governor Christopher Waller hinted at potential rate cuts in July. 

S&P 500 (SP500) +0.5%, the Dow (DJI) +0.4%, and the Nasdaq Composite (COMP:IND) +0.7%.

The 10-year Treasury yield (US10Y) rose 3 basis points to 4.42%. The 2-year yield (US2Y) rose 1 basis point to 3.95%.

Today also marks the second triple witching event of the year—a day on which $6.5T worth of contracts tied to stock options, stock index futures and stock index options will expire. Such days have typically seen volatility in equity markets.

Waller said Friday he sees little risk that tariffs will stoke inflation and signaled the central bank could start cutting interest rates as soon as July.

President Donald Trump is expected to decide within two weeks on a possible U.S. strike on Iran amid the latter's conflict with Israel. Investors took some relief from this after talks of the U.S. entering the tensions hurt sentiments. 

"Markets are recovering a bit this morning after White House spokeswoman Leavitt last night said that Trump had dictated a message saying that 'based on the fact that there's a substantial chance of negotiations that may or may not take place with Iran in the near future, I will make my decision whether or not to go within the next two weeks,'" Deutsche Bank's Jim Reid said. 

That means two weeks of uncertainty for financial markets, but investors are still inclined to see the Middle East conflict as a local, not a global, economic issue, UBS' Paul Donovan said. 

Wall Street on Wednesday erased modest gains to end little changed, after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said uncertainty stemming from tariffs continued to call for a "wait-and-see" approach to monetary policy actions.

The Fed left its policy rate at 4.25%-4.50% on Wednesday for a fourth straight meeting. 

The U.S. stock market was closed on Thursday to observe Juneteenth, after the NYSE and Nasdaq adopted the day as a new market holiday in 2022.

Turning to the economic side, the Philly Fed Manufacturing gauge stalled at -4 in June, missing consensus.

The May U.S. Leading Index will be released during market hours and is forecasted to come in at -0.1% M/M.

The Fed's balance sheet will land later in the day.

data.title: Apollo-backed Athora in talks for acquisition of UK-based Pension Insurance Corporation - report
data.description:
Apollo (NYSE:APO)-backed savings and retirement services group, Athora, is in discussions to acquire control of the specialist UK insurer Pension Insurance Corporation, Sky News reported on Friday.

Bankers estimate the deal could be worth between £4B and £5B, representing a landmark transaction in the pension risk transfer market, the report noted.

The details about the potential deal, including its pricing and structure, were reportedly unclear.

The U.S. private equity firm was said to have been considering a bid for PIC in 2023 as well, along with KKR (KKR) and Carlyle Group (CG).

data.title: Robin Energy stock slides after pricing $4M offering
data.description:
• Shares of Robin Energy (NASDAQ:RBNE) fell over 30% premarket on Friday after the shipping company priced a stock offering to raise around $4M.
  • The offering consists of 763,000 common shares, issued at a price of $5.25.
  • Robin Energy currently intends to use the net proceeds from the sale for working capital and general corporate purposes.
  • The offering is expected to close on or about June 20, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.
  • Source: Press Release

data.title: FinVolution Group prices $130M convertible notes offering
data.description:
FinVolution Group (NYSE:FINV) has pricing of its offering of convertible senior notes in an aggregate principal amount of US$130M due 2030.

The notes will bear interest at 2.50% per year, payable semi-annually, and will mature on July 1, 2030 unless repurchased, redeemed, or converted. The initial conversion rate is 80.8865 ADSs per US$1,000 principal amount of the notes, which is equivalent to an initial conversion price of ~$12.36 per ADS.

The company has granted the initial purchasers an option to purchase up to an additional $20M of the notes, exercisable for settlement within a 13-day period beginning on, and including, the date on which the notes are first issued.

FinVolution plans to use the net proceeds from the offering for international business expansions, replenishment of working capital, and funding the concurrent repurchase of 6.37M ADSs from certain purchasers of the notes.

The offering is expected to close on or about June 24, 2025, subject to the satisfaction of customary closing conditions.

Source: Press Release

data.title: ArcelorMittal nixes plans for two green steel plants in Germany due to high energy costs
data.description:
ArcelorMittal (NYSE:MT) said it canceled plans to convert two plants in Germany to carbon-neutral steel production because the country's energy costs were too high, Reuters reported Friday, in the latest blow to Germany's industrial sector.

The German government had hoped €1.3B in subsidies would encourage ArcelorMittal (NYSE:MT) to convert existing plants at Bremen in the north and Eisenhuettenstadt in the east to use furnaces fired with hydrogen, which can be created from renewably generated electricity.

But the company said it will not move forward with the plans because energy costs in Germany were too high and there was too much uncertainty about the country's future energy mix.

"The first electric arc forges are being built in countries that can offer competitive and predictable electricity provision," ArcelorMittal (MT) said, noting its recent investment in an electricity-fueled forge in nuclear-powered France.

"Electricity prices in Germany are high both by international standards and compared to neighbouring countries," the company also said.

Three other projects - by Salzgitter, Thyssenkrupp and Stahl-Holding-Saar - remain under way, the German economy ministry said.
